item.title: backlog list --status and --type flags missing validate_arg for empty values
item.intent: backlog list must reject flag calls with missing values — silently accepting an empty filter silently changes which items are shown, leading to misleading output.
item.description: In cmd_backlog_list(), the --status and --type flag cases do 'filter_status="$2"; shift 2' without calling validate_arg first. If a user runs 'backlog list --status' without a value, $2 is empty, filter_status gets set to empty string, and the subsequent jq filter produces unexpected results. cmd_backlog_add and cmd_backlog_edit consistently call validate_arg for all value-bearing flags. The fix is to add validate_arg calls in the --status and --type cases, consistent with the established pattern.
steps: In .aishore/aishore, find cmd_backlog_list() and locate the --status and --type flag cases (around line 3546-3552)
Add 'validate_arg "$1" "${2:-}" || return 1' before each assignment, matching the pattern in cmd_backlog_add/edit
Verify that 'backlog list --status' (no value) prints an error and exits non-zero
Verify that 'backlog list --type' (no value) prints an error and exits non-zero
Verify that 'backlog list --status todo' and 'backlog list --type bug' still work correctly
acceptanceCriteria: backlog list --status (no value) exits non-zero with an error message
backlog list --type (no value) exits non-zero with an error message
backlog list --status todo and backlog list --type bug still work correctly
